calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Of Relief From Provision Of Government Code 945.5, Filed April 12, 2019
Off Calendar Jul-31-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Petitioner JOSEPH ALVARADO (Tel: 415-564-0199) appearing in pro per;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PETITIONER JOSEPH ALVARADO's Petition For Order Of Relief From Provision Of Government Code 945.5, Filed April 12, 2019.

The Court notes the petitioner did not notify the Court or the opposing side of any intent to the contest the Court's tentative ruling. The petitioner addresses the Court. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Petitioner Alvarado's petition for relief from the claims presentation requirement is denied. First, the crux of plaintiff's claim relates to a fire that occurred in 2006. The court lacks the power to grant relief because plaintiff's application to present a late claim was presented to respondent more than a year after the incident. (See Government Code 946.6(c); 911.4(b).) Second, the petition does not articulate a clear basis for relief. To obtain relief, petitioner must show that the failure to present the claim was through mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect. (See Santee v. Santa Clara County Office of Education (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 702, 708; Gov't Code 946.6(c).) The petition fails to address this issue.

The prevailing party to prepare and submit a proposed order to the court that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185, E-MAIL: tonyvaughn@yahoo.com (302/EPS)
